description: Redwood Atlas expected-behavior matrix. Every check id MUST be covered by an executed assertion in tests/integration/redwood-atlas-simulation.test.ts (the test fails if any id is uncovered). Mechanisms: corpus = deterministic fixture-integrity check; rights = the production computeDecisionRights engine; sched = the production evaluateMeetingProposal engine; db = real Postgres seeding + governed retrieval.

checks.expect: corpus holds 40-60 artifacts spanning all 8 weeks
20+ statement-bearing conversation artifacts (meeting transcripts + call notes)
every statement carries speaker, role, ISO timestamp, text, and a communication act — who said what, when, as what act
every statement act is drawn from the 16-act vocabulary; no invented acts
all 16 communication acts are exercised somewhere in the corpus (including rejection and superseding_decision)
statement timestamps are valid ISO, on the artifact's date, and non-decreasing within an artifact
every speaker resolves to a person fixture or a named client contact — no anonymous authority
every internal decision/approval/assignment names an authority_basis found in that person's owns or can_approve rights — no decision without decision rights
client-side approvals are attributed to a named client key contact, never to an internal rights entry
commitments made outside authority are explicitly flagged exceeds_authority (Theo's full-automation promise)
every supersedes/superseded_by reference resolves to a real artifact id
every artifact with a superseded_by pointer carries non-current currentness (superseded/stale/contradicted)
all 8 conflict patterns are present in the corpus, each with build-up AND resolution artifacts (2+ artifacts per pattern)
every simulated document is labeled seeded_google_doc_simulation in its lineage — simulation never masquerades as a live connector
the superseding August 7 decision is OLDER-than-nothing: the superseded July 24 brief is not the newest artifact, and recency alone never restores it (currentness governs)
every artifact carries decision_makers_present and decision_rights_basis — authority context is never implicit
documentation catches up to communication: the v2 ownership matrix postdates and cites the reassignment decision transcript
participants carry per-person IANA timezones; the org spans PT/MT/ET/CT
authorized superseding decision (Maya + client COO, finalized, evidence strong) -> decision_made, high confidence, autonomy unblocked
the stale July 24 target (no finalized decision, weak evidence) -> autonomy blocked; never treated as decided
sales promise contradicting the approved scope owner -> disagreement_unresolved, low confidence, autonomy blocked, escalation to the scope owner
the authorized scope correction (Priya, finalized, aligned evidence) -> decision_made, high confidence
client daily-sync request with an internal expert objection and no decision -> blocked; a request is not policy
the finalized twice-weekly cadence decision -> decision_made, high confidence
manager 'ship Friday' vs the engineering owner's dependency evidence -> disagreement_unresolved, low, blocked, note names the expert's conflicting evidence
after the feasibility gate clears and the owner commits, the date decision -> decision_made, high confidence
CEO 'let's do it' above the finance threshold with policy NOT satisfied -> needs_authority_decision, blocked, escalation to the policy approver — policy outranks hierarchy
the joint Maya+Aisha approval satisfying policy -> decision_made, high confidence
the reassignment decision by the routing owner -> decision_made; routing follows the LATEST valid assignment, not the older matrix
expert evidence strong but no decision owner confirmation -> decision_proposed, medium, blocked — expertise alone does not finalize
Theo's 8AM-Pacific proposal is rejected for Pacific attendees with their local time named, while his 11AM EDT reads fine
every attendee verdict carries a timezone-labeled local time — who is affected, in their own local words
a conforming alternative inside everyone's working hours is proposed
the proposal note says proposed-times-only and never claims an event was created — connector truth
a 12:15 PT working-lunch proposal is flagged as a lunch-block conflict by default — the casual 'lunch is fine' chat never moved policy
lunch conflicts are per-person local: the ET attendee at 3:15 PM is not flagged
weekend proposals refuse with not-a-working-day
a clean in-hours slot across PT/MT/ET passes with an empty conflict summary
seeding the July 24 brief and the August 7 decision log, then marking the brief contradicted: retrieval SUPPRESSES the stale brief
the confirmed August 7 decision log outranks unvalidated background in governed retrieval order
retrieval stays read-only: no audits, capsules, notifications, or ledger rows change
every retrieval result carries should_not_act — retrieval informs, never acts
seeded simulation docs surface with attribution and never as live-connector truth (source label present, no 'Otzar knows')
the codified ranking law holds: live work > corrections > approved > confirmed > candidates > unvalidated > historical > suppressed
